m_b.integer: The rank must be a number
m_b.select_trial: Select a Team Trial series from the list

main_menu.info:
Want to help and see your artwork here?
Contact the developer!
main_menu.add_uma_info: Add a new Uma to the database
main_menu.add_trial_info: Add a new Team Trials race series
main_menu.show_results_info: View detailed results of your Umas
main_menu.choose_team_info:
Set your current TT team lineup
to speed up result entry.
main_menu.lang_info:
Change language.
Restart the program to apply changes.
main_menu.author: Author
main_menu.license_info:
Click to read the GNU GPL license
on the official website.
main_menu.github_info: Click to visit the GitHub program repository.

uma_add.info:
Add an Uma to the database by entering
her name and rank, and assigning
a distance category.
uma_add.name: Name
uma_add.rank_info:
Give a numeric score without commas (16150),
not a rank (UG1)!

add_trial.title: Add Team Trials
add_trial.info:
Add a Team Trials to the database by entering
its date and time.
add_trial.hour_format_btn:
Select time format.
To select 'PM', type 'p' after the hour.
To select 'AM', remove 'P' from 'PM'.
This setting will be saved.
add_trial.check_info:
Check this to keep the date field filled
after adding a trials.
This setting will be remembered.

add_results.title: Add Results
add_results.info:
Enter the Uma positions for the given Team Trials
and, optionally, their scores.
add_results.help:
Select the Team Trials series from
the dropdown list on the right,
then enter the finishing positions as integers.

actual_team.title: Actual Team
actual_team.info:
Assign your Umas to a specific distance
by selecting them from the lists.
actual_team.info2:
Note that an Uma is assigned to a specific distance.
If you want to assign her to another, add her
as a new one in the target distance.
actual_team.delete_txt: Click the position label to clear the field.

help.title: Help
help.welcome:
Welcome to UmaTT-db!
Please read this help guide to use the program comfortably.
help.first: The first step should be adding a new Uma to the database.
help.first2: To do this, click 'Add Uma' in the main menu.
help.first3:
Enter her name, rating,
and assign a distance from the dropdown list.
help.first5:
The Uma will be saved in the database.
It will be assigned a unique ID, so it is treated
as a unique character. If you want to enter a better version
of the same Uma, simply add it as a new one.
help.set1:
After adding a few Umas, set up the current team lineup
you are using in-game.
help.set2:
Assign an Uma by selecting her from the list
to the corresponding distance and position.
help.set3:
If you want to clear a specific field, simply
click the position shortcut (e.g., 'PC').
help.set4: Once all Umas are set, click 'Save Team'.
help.set5:
Visit this window whenever you make
changes to your team.
help.trial1:
After adding Umas and setting the squad,
you can add a Team Trials race.
help.trial2: Enter the date and the exact time.
help.trial3: The 'Today' button will instantly fill in the current date.
help.trial4:
The time format button and the 'Don't clear' checkbox
will retain their settings.
help.trial5: No need to enter any symbols; they will be added automatically.
help.trial6:
After entering the data, save the race by clicking
'Add Trial' or pressing Enter.
help.trial7:
Enter all current races at once - for example: the 5 latest ones.
This will speed up the workflow.
help.results1:
The next step is to enter the results
for your Umas. This window loads your current team lineup.
help.results2:
First, select the TT race you want to add results to
from the top dropdown list.
We recommend choosing the oldest available race - the one
you added first in the previous window.
help.results3:
Next, enter the positions for all Umas.
Enter them as integers (e.g., '1').
Click 'Save Results'.
help.results4:
The program will save all data to the database and automatically
switch to the next (newer) race. If none remain,
the field will appear empty.
help.view1:
This is the most important window of the program.
Here you can track and analyze your Umas' results.
help.view2:
On the top left is a list of all your Umas.
After selecting one, you can see her progress on the chart.
help.view3:
The top list shows distances; after selecting one,
the chart will display all Umas assigned to it.
help.view4:
The top right list displays specific Team Trials races.
After selecting one, the chart will show the positions of all Umas
who participated in that particular race.
help.view5:
Above the 'Back to Menu' button and checkbox, there is a box
indicating the currently selected value from the lists.
help.view6:
Right above the button is a checkbox that, when checked, filters
Umas from the distance and race lists based on their presence in your current squad.
When enabled, it displays only the Umas currently in your squad.
You must re-select a value from the lists for it to take effect.
This checkbox does not affect the Uma list.
help.view7:
The chart itself highlights first, second, and third places.
The vertical axis represents the position achieved, while the horizontal axis displays
a specific TT race (for the Uma and distance lists)
or a specific Uma (for the TT race list).
help.summary1:
In the main menu, you will find a link to this project's
GitHub repository. I encourage you to check it out, leave comments,
and suggest fixes, changes, or new features!
help.summary2:
I hope using this program proves fruitful and pleasant,
bringing you success in your UmaMusume: Pretty Derby gameplay.
